questionText: Break down your gross Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by subsidiary.
questionKo: 총 Scope 1 및 Scope 2 배출량을 자회사별로 구분하여 입력하십시오.
sectorTags: Financial services, Oil & Gas, Coal, General, Aviation, Capital goods, Transport OEMS - EPM, Chemicals, Metals & mining, Transport services, Steel, Agricultural commodities, Transport OEMS, Energy utilities & power generators, Cement, Food, beverage & tobacco, Paper & forestry, Real estate, Construction
simpleHelp: Awareness of subsidiary-level emissions enables a parent organization to target actions to reduce emissions. The breakdown also provides data users with the opportunity to better understand the emissions sources and therefore risks and opportunities throughout the business.

options: Pharma & health care supplies wholesale & distribution
optionsKo: Pharma & health care supplies wholesale & distribution

columns.text: Select the unique identifier you are able to provide for this subsidiary
columns.popup_help: "No unique identifier" cannot be selected alongside any other option
columns.popup_helpKo: "No unique identifier" cannot be 선택 alongside 하나 이상 other option

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Yes" in response to 7.23.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "Yes" in response to 7.23.

guidance.text:
General 
Add a new row for each subsidiary you are providing emissions data for. You should aim to break down your Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by all subsidiaries that fall within the boundary determined by your consolidation approach (reported in 6.1). But note that the total emissions entered will not be cross-checked by CDP for consistency with your total emissions reported in 7.6 and 7.7.
Organizations should consistently apply the consolidation approach they have detailed in 6.1 to their subsidiaries. E.g. If using an equity share approach, you should disclose your subsidiaries' emissions according to this approach. This should be explained in the ‘Comment' column.
If you are not able to provide a breakdown for all subsidiaries, please ensure that you have included at a minimum any subsidiaries that were requested to disclose to investors for the reporting year.
If no subsidiaries were requested to disclose to CDP in the reporting year, it is up to your organization to decide which subsidiaries to report on e.g. reporting on the subsidies that represent the largest proportion of emissions.
Emissions must be reported in gross, not net figures. Therefore, negative numbers are not allowed.
Emissions figures should be for the reporting year only (as defined by your answer to 1.4).
If you are unable to provide either a Scope 1 or Scope 2 emissions figure for a subsidiary, leave the relevant column blank and do not enter zero.
Entering zero indicates that you have measured the emissions and that they are equal to zero.
Subsidiary name (column 1) 
Provide the full legal entity name of the subsidiary you are entering data for.
The subsidiary name provided should be in its original language, not a translation.
Primary activity (column 2) 
Select the option that best describes the primary activity from which the subsidiary derives revenue. If the subsidiary engages in multiple activities, select the activity from which it derives the greatest share of its revenue.
For a full list of classifications including descriptions of each activity, see CDP's Activity Classification System .
Select the unique identifier(s) you are able to provide for this subsidiary (column 3) 
If your subsidiary organization has multiple unique identifiers, select all the unique identifiers you are able to provide.
For each unique identifier selected, column 11 will appear for you to enter the unique identifier.
Ensure that you enter the correct format for the unique identifier(s) you are providing. For example, ISIN codes include a two-letter country code, followed by a nine-character alphanumeric identifier and a single check digit.
Scope 2, market-based emissions (metric tons CO2e) (column 14) 
This column only appears if you select "We are reporting a Scope 2, market-based figure" in column "Scope 2, market-based" of 7.3.
Comment (column 15) 
You can use this text field to enter any additional relevant information. For example, you may wish to provide context to the subsidiaries you have included within your response to this question, based on your chosen consolidation approach.
guidance.textKo: 일반 Add a 신규 행 for each subsidiary you are providing 배출량 데이터 for. You 해야 합니다 aim to break down your Scope 1 및 Scope 2 배출량 by all subsidiaries that fall within the boundary determined by your consolidation approach (보고 in 6.1). But note that the total emissions entered will not be cross-checked by CDP for consistency with your total emissions 보고 in 7.6 및 7.7. Organizations 해야 합니다 consistently apply the consolidation approach they have detailed in 6.1 to their subsidiaries. E.g. 만약 using an equity share approach, you 해야 합니다 disclose your subsidiaries' emissions according to this approach. This 해야 합니다 be explained in the ‘Ticker 기호' 열. 만약 you are not able to provide a breakdown for all subsidiaries, please ensure that you have included at a 최소 하나 이상 subsidiaries that were requested to disclose to investors 에 대한 보고연도. 만약 no subsidiaries were requested to disclose to CDP in the 보고연도, it is up to 귀사 to decide which subsidiaries to report on e.g. reporting on the subsidies that represent the largest proportion of emissions. Emissions 해야 합니다 be 보고 in gross, not net figures. Therefore, negative numbers are not allowed. Emissions figures 해야 합니다 be 에 대한 보고연도 only (as defined by your answer to 1.4). 만약 you are unable to provide either a Scope 1 또는 Scope 2 배출량 figure for a subsidiary, leave the 관련 열 blank 및 do not enter zero. Entering zero indicates that you have measured the emissions 및 that they are equal to zero. 자회사 이름 (열 1) Provide the full legal entity name 의 subsidiary you are entering data for. The 자회사 이름 입력 해야 합니다 be in its original language, not a translation. SEDOL 코드 (열 2) Select the option that best 설명s the SEDOL 코드 from which the subsidiary derives 매출. 만약 the subsidiary engages in multiple activities, select the activity from which it derives the greatest share of its 매출. For a full list of classifications 포함 descriptions of each activity, see CDP's Activity Classification System. Select the unique identifier(s) you are able to provide for this subsidiary (열 3) 만약 your subsidiary organization has multiple unique identifiers, select all the unique identifiers you are able to provide. For each unique identifier 선택, 열 11 will appear for you to enter the unique identifier. Ensure that you enter the correct format 에 대한 unique identifier(s) you are providing. For example, ISIN codes include a two-letter country code, followed by a nine-character alphanumeric identifier 및 a single check digit. Scope 2, 시장기반 emissions (미터톤 CO2e) (열 14) This 열 only appears 만약 you select "We are reporting a Scope 2, 시장기반 figure" in 열 "Scope 2, 시장기반" of 7.3. Ticker 기호 (열 15) You can use this text field to enter 하나 이상 additional 관련 information. For example, you may wish to provide context to the subsidiaries you have included within your response to this 문항, based on your chosen consolidation approach.

guidance.text: Companies are transparent about their emissions inventories, including their subsidiaries.
guidance.textKo: Companies are transparent about their emissions inventories, 포함 their subsidiaries.

guidance.text:
CUSIP number:  Committee on Uniform Security Identification Procedures number, a 9-character alphanumeric code that identifies a security for the purposes of facilitating clearing and settlement of trades. CUSIPs are used to distinguish, among other reasons, between multiple share classes or bond tranches. CUSIPs are mostly used in the United States and Canada. 
D-U-N-S: Dun & Bradstreet Universal Numbering System, a system of nine-digit numerical identifiers assigned by Dun & Bradstreet to business entities. The D-U-N-S number is used to establish and maintain a standardized business profile and is widely used for business credit reports.
ISIN:  International Securities Identification Number, a 12-character alphanumeric code used to identify a security, such as a stock or bond. It is structured with the first two letters referencing the country of origin of the issuer for the security, in accordance with ISO 3166. The second grouping consists of nine characters made up of digits and letters, which is the unique identifying code for the security. In the U.S. and Canada this is known as the CUSIP number (see below). The final digit is the check digit, which ensures the authenticity of the code. 
LEI number:  The Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) is a 20-character, alpha-numeric code based on the ISO 17442 standard developed by the International Organization for Standardization (ISO). It connects to key reference information that enables clear and unique identification of legal entities participating in financial transactions. 
SEDOL code:  Stock Exchange Daily Official List code, a 7-character identification code consisting of two parts: a 6-character alphanumeric code and a trailing check digit. SEDOLs issued prior to January 26, 2004 were composed only of numbers. SEDOLs serve as the National Securities Identifying Number for all securities issued in the United Kingdom. 
Ticker symbol:  A ticker symbol, also known as a stock symbol, is a unique series of letters assigned to a security for trading purposes. Ticker symbols are usually related to the organization's name, and additional letters denote additional characteristics such as share class or trading restrictions.
guidance.textKo: Scope 1 배출량(metric tons CO2e): 위원회 on Uniform Security Identification 절차s number, a 9-character alphanumeric code that identifies a security 에 대한 purposes of facilitating clearing 및 settlement of trades. CUSIPs are used to distinguish, among other reasons, between multiple share classes 또는 bond tranches. CUSIPs are mostly used in the United States 및 Canada. D-U-N-S: Dun & Bradstreet Universal Numbering System, a system of nine-digit numerical identifiers assigned by Dun & Bradstreet to business entities. The 주요 활동 is used to establish 및 maintain a standardized business profile 및 is widely used for business credit reports. ISIN: International Securities Identification Number, a 12-character alphanumeric code used to identify a security, such as a stock 또는 bond. It is structured with the first two letters referencing the country of origin 의 issuer 에 대한 security, in accordance with ISO 3166. The second grouping consists of nine characters made up of digits 및 letters, which is the unique identifying code 에 대한 security. In the U.S. 및 Canada this is known as the Scope 1 배출량(metric tons CO2e) (see below). The final digit is the check digit, which ensures the authenticity 의 code. 비고: The Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) is a 20-character, alpha-numeric code based on the ISO 17442 standard developed by the International Organization for Standardization (ISO). It connects to key reference information that enables clear 및 unique identification of legal entities participating in financial transactions. Scope 2, 시장기반 배출량 (metric tons CO2e): Stock Exchange Daily Official List code, a 7-character identification code consisting of two parts: a 6-character alphanumeric code 및 a trailing check digit. SEDOLs issued prior to January 26, 2004 were composed only of numbers. SEDOLs serve as the National Securities Identifying Number for all securities issued in the United Kingdom. Scope 2, 지역기반 배출량(metric tons CO2e): A Scope 2, 지역기반 배출량(metric tons CO2e), also known as a stock symbol, is a unique series of letters assigned to a security for trading purposes. Scope 2, 지역기반 배출량(metric tons CO2e)s are usually related to the 조직의 name, 및 additional letters denote additional characteristics such as share class 또는 trading restrictions.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of rows disclosed in the following columns:
- 'Subsidiary name'
- 'Scope 1 emissions (metric tons CO2e)'
- Either 'Scope 2, location-based emissions (metric tons CO2e)' OR 'Scope 2, market-based emissions (metric tons CO2e)'
A maximum of 3/3 points is available in this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성된 칸(셀) in proportion to the number of 행 공개 in the following 열: - '자회사 이름' - 'Scope 1 배출량 (미터톤 CO2e)' - Either 'Scope 2, 지역기반 emissions (미터톤 CO2e)' 또는 'Scope 2, 시장기반 emissions (미터톤 CO2e)' A 최고 of 3/3 점 is available in this 문항.

criteria.criteriaKo: 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성된 칸(셀) in proportion to the number of 행 공개 in the following 열: - '자회사 이름' - 'Scope 1 배출량 (미터톤 CO2e)' - Either 'Scope 2, 지역기반 emissions (미터톤 CO2e)' 또는 'Scope 2, 시장기반 emissions (미터톤 CO2e)' A 최고 of 3/3 점 is available in this 문항.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of rows disclosed in the following columns:
- 'Subsidiary name'
- 'Scope 1 emissions (metric tons CO2e)'
- Either 'Scope 2, location-based emissions (metric tons CO2e)' OR 'Scope 2, market-based emissions (metric tons CO2e)'
A maximum of 3/3 points is available in this question.

simpleHelpKo: 인지 of subsidiary-level emissions enables a parent organization to 목표 actions to reduce emissions. The breakdown also provides 데이터 사용자 with the opportunity to better understand the emissions sources 및 therefore 리스크 및 기회 throughout the business.
